entities.description: As a subsection of 'Graph Construction' within the 'BOOKINDEX' chapter, this section details the specific algorithm for populating the Knowledge Graph by iterating through tree nodes and extracting subgraphs based on content modality (text or visual).
The structured data repository being constructed in this section, populated by extracting entities and relations from document tree nodes.
The hierarchical structure previously established that serves as the source of nodes to be processed for graph construction.
Large Language Model used as a tool to extract entities and relations when processing text-only nodes.
VLM employed specifically to extract visual knowledge from nodes containing image elements.
A specific node type indicating the presence of visual elements requiring VLM-based extraction.
The final mapping structure constructed by recording the origin tree node for every extracted entity.

relations.description: The Knowledge Graph is the primary object being constructed in this section.
The Tree T provides the input nodes that are iterated over during the construction process.
The LLM is the method/tool utilized for extracting data from text-only nodes within this section.
The Vision Language Model is the method/tool utilized for extracting data from visual nodes within this section.
The Image node type triggers the use of the Vision Language Model in this section's logic.
The Mapping M is the critical output artifact generated by recording entity origins in this section.
